steps.title: Interpret the treatment response
steps.content: After a simulated attack, caterpillars exposed to harmless electric field shifts stayed defensive for many seconds. In the control group, caterpillars resumed neutral behavior in less than one-tenth of a second. The treatment response suggests the field shifts were detected as threat cues.

steps.title: Stay within the evidence
steps.content: Choice B depends on different shift sizes, which were not tested. Choice C ranks species sensitivity, which the study does not establish. Choice D invents an ability to cause field shifts. The remaining choice directly explains the defensive response. The answer is A.
